categories.pearl: Thermafil/GuttaCore: plastic (or GP) carrier coated with alpha-phase GP. Heated and inserted to WL. Alpha-phase GP flows at lower temperatures than beta, better adapts to canal walls. Disadvantage: carrier cannot be removed for retreatment (use solvent + rotary retreatment files).

categories.name: Single-Cone (Sealer-Based) Obturation
categories.pearl: Sealer-based obturation: bioceramic sealer (BC Sealer, Total Fill) placed into canal → matched taper GP cone placed to WL. Sealer fills irregularities hydraulically. Reliance on sealer is the key paradigm shift — requires adequate canal preparation to 'clean and shape' before sealer placement. Poor preparation → voids in sealer layer.

categories.name: Obturation in Curved Canals
categories.pearl: Curved canals: warm vertical compaction most forgiving — plugger pre-curved to match canal. Carrier-based: carrier naturally follows canal shape if heated appropriately. Lateral compaction in curved canals → risk of straightening curve and transporting fill. Confirm master cone length with PA before obturation in all curved canals.

categories.name: Overfill: When It Matters and When It Doesn't
categories.pearl: Overfill prognosis depends heavily on material. ZOE overfill: cytotoxic, persistent symptoms, worse outcomes (eugenol tissue irritation). Epoxy resin overfill: irritating initially, usually resorbs or walled off, acceptable in most cases. Bioceramic/MTA overfill: best biocompatibility, forms HA interface with bone. GP in healthy tissue: generally tolerated, resorbs slowly.

categories.name: Biofilm Formation Stages
categories.pearl: Four-stage model: 1) Reversible attachment — planktonic bacteria adhere to surface (pellicle). 2) Irreversible — type IV pili, other adhesins anchor bacteria. 3) Microcolony + EPS matrix production. 4) Dispersal — bacteria shed from mature biofilm to seed new sites. Stage 3 is most resistant to antimicrobials.

categories.name: Quorum Sensing in Endodontic Biofilm
categories.pearl: Quorum sensing (QS): bacteria produce autoinducer molecules (AHLs, AI-2) → at threshold concentration, coordinate gene expression for biofilm formation, virulence factor production, and antibiotic resistance. Breaking QS with quorum-quenching compounds or furanones is an active research area for biofilm management in endo.

categories.name: EPS Matrix Components
categories.pearl: EPS matrix: ~75% water by volume (water channels allow nutrient/waste exchange). Polysaccharides provide structural scaffold. Extracellular DNA (eDNA, released from lysed cells) contributes to biofilm stability and promotes horizontal gene transfer. eDNA and polysaccharides together are primary barriers to antimicrobial penetration.

categories.name: Endodontic Biofilm Location in Failed Cases
categories.pearl: Sundqvist's seminal work: bacteria persist in the apical 3 mm regardless of coronal debridement technique. Lateral canals and isthmus areas harbor surviving biofilm. Dentinal tubule invasion: bacteria penetrate 0.1-1 mm (rarely deeper than 2 mm). Extraradicular biofilm forms on cementum surface, inaccessible to irrigants — requires surgery.

categories.name: Apical Anatomy Terminology
categories.pearl: Minor foramen = apical constriction (narrowest point inside root canal, ~0.5-1 mm from apex). Major foramen = where canal exits root. Apical delta: complex network of small canals at apex (common in lower anterior teeth). CDJ ~ 0.5-1 mm from minor foramen; the biological and recommended obturation terminus.

categories.name: Apical Foramen Location Variations
categories.pearl: The apical foramen is frequently NOT at the radiographic apex. Green (1955): lateral wall exit in 17-46% depending on tooth type. Multiple accessory foramina (apical delta) more common in mandibular incisors and maxillary premolars. CBCT: identifies the true foramen position for surgical planning.

categories.name: Apical Preparation Size Controversy
terms: Minimum: 3-4 sizes larger than initial file
categories.pearl: No single apical preparation size is universally correct. Recommendation: prepare 3-4 sizes larger than the first binding file at WL (Coldero). Canals with smaller initial diameter (e.g., immature premolars): larger relative preparation needed. Master apical file (MAF): should have tactile resistance at WL — not pass freely.

categories.name: Apical Patency Controversy
terms: Patency file: #10 K-file to WL + 1 mm
categories.pearl: Apical patency (Buchanan): passing small file (≤#10) through the foramen between instrumentation to maintain its patency and prevent debris packing. Proponents: improves irrigation flow and prevents apical blockage. Opponents: risk of extruding infected material and destroying the apical constriction. No definitive RCT evidence on outcomes.
